author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter III. 29–36.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This lesson from the holy Gospel shows us the excellency of our Lord Jesus Christ’s divinity, and the humility of the man who earned the title of the Bridegroom’s friend; that we may distinguish between the man who is man, and the Man who is God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Man who is God is our Lord Jesus Christ, God before all ages, Man in the age of our world: God of the Father, man of the Virgin, yet one and the same Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ, Son of God, God and man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But John, a man of distinguished grace, was sent before Him, a man enlightened by Him who is the Light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For of John it is said, “He was not the Light, but that he should bear witness of the Light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He may himself be called a light indeed, and rightly so; but an enlightened, not an enlightening light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The light that enlightens, and that which is enlightened, are different things: for even our eyes are called lights (lumina), and yet when we open them in the dark, they do not see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the light that enlightens is a light both from itself and for itself, and does not need another light for its shining; but all the rest need it, that they may shine.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly John confessed Him: as you have heard that when Jesus was making many disciples, and they reported to John as if to excite him to jealousy,—for they told the matter as if moved by envy, “Lo, he is making more disciples than thou,”—John confessed what he was, and thereby merited to belong to Him, because he dared not affirm himself to be that which Jesus is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now this is what John said: “A man cannot receive anything, except it be given him from heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore Christ gives, man receives.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Ye yourselves bear me witness that I said, I am not the Christ, but that I am sent before Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that hath the bride is the Bridegroom; but the friend of the Bridegroom, who standeth and heareth Him, rejoiceth greatly because of the Bridegroom’s voice.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not of himself did he give himself joy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that will have joy of himself shall be sad; but he that will have his joy of God will ever rejoice, because God is everlasting.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou desire to have everlasting joy?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Cleave to Him who is everlasting.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such an one John declared himself to be.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because of the Bridegroom’s voice, the friend of the Bridegroom rejoiceth,” not because of his own voice, and “standeth and heareth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, if he falls, he heareth Him not: for of a certain one who fell it is said, “And he stood not in the truth;” this is said of the devil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It behoves the Bridegroom’s friend, then, “to stand and to hear.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is it to stand?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is to abide in His grace, which he received.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And he hears a voice at which he rejoices.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such was John: he knew whereof he rejoiced; he did not arrogate to himself to be what he was not; he knew himself as one enlightened, not the enlightener.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But that was the true Light,” saith the evangelist, “that lighteneth every man coming into this world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If “every man,” then also John himself; for he too is of men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, although none hath arisen among them that are born of women greater than John, yet he was himself one of those that are born of women.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is he to be compared with Him who, because He willed it, was born by a singular and extraordinary birth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For both generations of the Lord are unexampled, both the divine and the human: by the divine He has no mother; by the human, no father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore John was but one of the rest: of greater grace, however, so that of those born of women none arose greater than he; so great a testimony he gave to our Lord Jesus Christ as to call Him the Bridegroom, and himself the Bridegroom’s friend, not worthy however to loose the latchet of the Bridegroom’s shoe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have already heard much on this point, beloved: let us look to what follows; for it is somewhat hard to understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But as John himself says, that “no man can receive anything, except it be given him from heaven,” whatever we shall not have understood, let us ask Him who gives from heaven: for we are men, and cannot receive anything, except He, who is not man, give it us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now this is what follows: and John says, “This my joy therefore is fulfilled.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is his joy?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To rejoice at the Bridegroom’s voice.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is fulfilled in me, I have my grace; more I do not assume to myself, lest also I lose what I have received.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this joy?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “With joy rejoiceth for the Bridegroom’s voice.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A man may understand, then, that he ought not to rejoice of his own wisdom, but of the wisdom which he has received from God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him ask nothing more, and he loses not what he found.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For many, in that they affirmed themselves to be wise, became fools.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The apostle convicts them, and says of them, “Because that which is known of God is manifest to them; for God has showed it unto them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear ye what he says of certain unthankful, ungodly men: “For the invisible things of Him from the creation of the world are seen, being understood by the things that are made, His eternal power likewise, and Godhead; so that they are without excuse.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why without excuse?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because, knowing God” (he said not, “because they knew Him not”), “they glorified Him not as God, nor were thankful; but became vain in their imaginations, and their foolish heart was darkened: professing themselves to be wise, they became fools.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If they had known God, they had known at the same time that God, and none other, had made them wise; and they would not then attribute to themselves that which they did not have from themselves, but to Him from whom they had received it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But by their unthankfulness they became fools.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, what God gave freely, He took from the unthankful.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John would not be this; he would be thankful: he confessed to have received, and declared that he rejoiced for the Bridegroom’s voice, saying, “Therefore this my joy is fulfilled.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He must increase, but I must decrease.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He must be exalted, but I must be humbled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How is Jesus to increase?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How is God to increase?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The perfect does not increase.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God neither increases nor decreases.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He increases, He is not perfect; if He decreases, he is not God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how can Jesus increase, being God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If to man’s estate, since He deigned to be man and was a child; and, though the Word of God, lay an infant in a manger; and, though His mother’s Creator, yet sucked the milk of infancy of her: then Jesus having grown in age of the flesh, that perhaps is the reason why it is said, “He must increase, but I must decrease.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why in this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As regards the flesh, John and Jesus were of the same age, there being six months between them: they had grown up together; and if our Lord Jesus Christ had willed to be here longer before His death, and that John should be here with Him, then, as they had grown up together, so would they have grown old together: in what way, then, “He must increase but I must decrease”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Above all, our Lord Jesus Christ being now thirty years old, does a man who is already thirty years old still grow?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From that same age, men begin to go downward, and to decline to graver age, thence to old age.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again, even had they both been lads, he would not have said, “He must increase,” but, We must increase together.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now each is thirty years of age.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The interval of six months makes no difference in age; the difference is discovered by reading rather than by the look of the persons.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means, then, “He must increase, but I must decrease”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is a great mystery!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Before the Lord Jesus came, men were glorying of themselves; He came a man, to lessen man’s glory, and to increase the glory of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now He came without sin, and found all men in sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thus He came to put away sin, God may freely give, man may confess.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For man’s confession is man’s lowliness: God’s pity is God’s loftiness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, since He came to forgive man his sins, let man acknowledge his own lowliness and let God show His pity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He must increase, but I must decrease:” that is, He must give, but I must receive; He must be glorified, but I must confess.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let man know his own condition, and confess to God; and hear the apostle as he says to a proud, elated man, bent on extolling himself: “What hast thou that thou didst not receive?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if thou didst receive it, why dost thou glory as if thou didst not receive it?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then let man understand that he has received; and when he would call that his own which is not his, let him decrease: for it is good for him that God be glorified in him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him decrease in himself, that he may be increased in God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These testimonies and this truth, Christ and John signified by their deaths.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For John was lessened by the Head: Christ was exalted on the cross; so that even there it appeared what this is, “He must increase, but I must decrease.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again, Christ was born when the days were just beginning to lengthen; John was born when they began to shorten.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus their very creation and deaths testify to the words of John, when he says, “He must increase, but I must decrease.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May the glory of God then increase in us, and our own glory decrease, that even ours may increase in God!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this is what the apostle says, this is what Holy Scripture says: “He that glorieth, let him glory in the Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wilt thou glory in thyself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou wilt grow; but grow worse in thy evil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For whoso grows worse is justly decreased.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let God, then, who is ever perfect, grow, and grow in thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the more thou understandest God, and apprehendest Him, He seems to be growing in thee; but in Himself He grows not, being ever perfect.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou didst understand a little yesterday; thou understandest more to-day, wilt understand much more to-morrow: the very light of God increases in thee: as if thus God increases, who remains ever perfect.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is as if one’s eyes were being cured of former blindness, and he began to see a little glimmer of light, and the next day he saw more, and the third day still more: to him the light would seem to grow; yet the light is perfect, whether he see it or not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus it is also with the inner man: he makes progress indeed in God, and God seems to be increasing in him; yet man himself is decreasing, that he may fall from his own glory, and rise into the glory of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What we have just heard, appears now distinctly and clearly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that cometh from above, is above all.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See what he says of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What of himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that is of the earth, is of earth, and speaketh of the earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that cometh from above is above all”—this is Christ; and “he that is of the earth, is of earth, and speaketh of the earth”—this is John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And is this the whole: John is of the earth, and speaks of the earth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is the whole testimony that he bears of Christ a speaking of the earth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are they not voices of God that are heard from John, when he bears witness of Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then how does he speak of the earth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said this of man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So far as relates to man in himself, he is of earth, and speaks of the earth; and when he speaks some divine things, he is enlightened by God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, were he not enlightened, he would be earth speaking of earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God’s grace is apart by itself, the nature of man apart by itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do but examine the nature of man: man is born and grows, he learns the customs of men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does he know but earth, of earth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He speaks the things of men, knows the things of men, minds the things of men; carnal, he judges carnally, conjectures carnally: lo! it is man all over.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the grace of God come, and enlighten his darkness, as it saith, “Thou wilt lighten my candle, O Lord; my God, enlighten my darkness;” let it take the mind of man, and turn it to its own light; immediately he begins to say, as the apostle says, “Yet not I, but the grace of God that is with me;” and, “Now I live; yet not I, but Christ liveth in me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That is to say, “He must increase, but I must decrease.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus John: as regards John, he is of the earth, and speaks of the earth; whatever that is divine thou hast heard from John, is of Him that enlightens, not of him that receives.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that cometh from heaven is above all; and what He hath seen and heard, that He testifieth: and no man receiveth His testimony.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Cometh from heaven, is above all, our Lord Jesus Christ; of whom it was said above, “No man hath ascended into heaven, but He that came down from heaven, the Son of man who is in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He is above all; “and what He hath seen and heard, that He speaks.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, He hath a Father, being Himself the Son of God; He hath a Father, and He also hears of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what is that which He hears of the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who can unfold this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When can my tongue, when can my heart be sufficient, either the heart to understand, or the tongue to utter, what that is which the Son hath heard from the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May it be the Son has heard the Word of the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nay, the Son is the Word of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You see how all human effort is here wearied out; you see how all guessing of our heart, all straining of our darkened mind, here fails.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I hear the Scripture saying that the Son speaks that which He heareth from the Father; and again, I hear the Scripture saying that the Son is Himself the Word of the Father: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The words that we speak are fleeting and transient: as soon as thy word has sounded from thy mouth, it passeth away; it makes its noise, and passes away into silence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Canst thou follow thy sound, and hold it to make it stand?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thy thought, however, remains, and of that thought that remains thou utterest many words that pass away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What say we, brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When God spake, did He give out a voice, or sounds, or syllables?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He did, in what tongue spake He?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In Hebrew, or in Greek, or in Latin?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Tongues are necessary where there is a distinction of nations.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But there none can say that God spake in this tongue, or in that.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Observe thy own heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When thou conceivest a word which thou mayest utter,—For I will say, if I can, what we may note in ourselves, not whereby we may comprehend that,—well, when thou conceivest a word to utter, thou meanest to utter a thing, and the very conception of the thing is already a word in thy heart: it has not yet come forth, but it is already born in the heart, and is waiting to come forth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But thou considerest the person to whom it is to come forth, with whom thou art to speak: if he is a Latin, thou seekest a Latin expression; if a Greek, thou thinkest of Greek words; if a Punic, thou considerest whether thou knowest the Punic language: for the diversity of hearers thou hast recourse to divers tongues to utter the word conceived; but the conception itself was bound by no tongue in particular.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whilst therefore God, when speaking, required not a language, nor took up any kind of speech, how was He heard by the Son, seeing that God’s speaking is the Son Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As, in fact, thou hast in thy heart the word that thou speakest, and as it is with thee, and is none other than the spiritual conception itself (for just as thy soul is spirit, so also the word which thou hast conceived is spirit; for it has not yet received sound to be divided by syllables, but remains in the conception of thy heart, and in the mirror of the mind); so God gave out His Word, that is, begat the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And thou, indeed, begettest the word even in thy heart according to time; God without time begat the Son by whom He created all times.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whilst, therefore, the Son is the Word of God, and the Son spoke to us not His own word, but the word of the Father, He willed to speak Himself to us when He was speaking the word of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This it is that John said, as was fit and necessary; and we have expounded according to our ability.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He whose heart has not yet attained to a proper perception of so great a matter, has whither to turn himself, has where to knock, has from whom to ask, from whom to seek, of whom to receive.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that cometh from heaven is above all; and what He hath seen and heard, that testifieth He; and His testimony no man receiveth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If no man, to what purpose came He?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He means, no man of a certain class.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There are some people prepared for the wrath of God, to be damned with the devil; of these, none receiveth the testimony of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if none at all, not any man, received, what could these words mean, “But he that received His testimony hath set to his seal that God is true”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not certainly, then, no man, if thou sayest thyself, “He that received His testimony has set to his seal that God is true.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps John, on being questioned, would answer and say, I know what I have said, in saying no man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There are, in fact, people born to God’s wrath, and thereunto foreknown.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For God knows who they are that will and that will not believe; He knows who they are that shall persevere in that in which they have believed, and who that shall fall away; and all that shall be for eternal life are numbered by God; and He knows already the people set apart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if He knows this, and has given to the prophets by His Spirit to know it, He gave this also to John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now John was observing, not with his eye,—for as regards himself he is earth, and speaketh of earth,—but with that grace of the Spirit which he received of God, he saw a certain people, ungodly, unbelieving.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Contemplating that people in its unbelief, he says, “His testimony, who came from heaven, no man receiveth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No man of whom?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of them who shall be on the left hand, of them to whom it shall be said, “Go into the everlasting fire, which is prepared for the devil and his angels.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who are they that do receive it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They who shall be at the right hand, they to whom it shall be said, “Come, ye blessed of my Father, receive the kingdom which is prepared for you from the beginning of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He observes, then, in the Spirit a dividing, but in the human race a mingling together; and that which is not yet separated locally, he separated in the understanding, in the view of the heart; and he saw two peoples, one of believers, one of unbelievers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Fixing his thought on the unbelievers, he says, “He that cometh from heaven is above all; and what He hath seen and heard, that He testifieth and no man receiveth His testimony.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He then turned his thought from the left hand, and looked at the right, and proceeded to say, “He that received His testimony has set to his seal that God is true.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means “has set to his seal that God is true,” if it be not that man is a liar, and God is true?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For no human being can speak any truth, unless he be enlightened by Him who cannot lie.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God, then, is true; but Christ is God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wouldest thou prove this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Receive His testimony and thou findest it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “he that hath received His testimony has set to his seal that God is true.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who is true?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The same who came from heaven, and is above all, is God, and true.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if thou dost not yet understand Him to be God, thou hast not yet received His testimony: receive it, and thou puttest thy seal to it; confidently thou understandest, definitely thou acknowledgest, that God is true.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For He whom God hath sent speaketh the words of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Himself is the true God, and God sent Him: God sent God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Join both, one God, true God sent by God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ask concerning them singly, He is God; ask concerning them both, they are God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not individually God, and both Gods; but each individual God, and both God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For so great is the charity of the Holy Spirit there, so great the peace of unity, that when thou questionest about them individually, the answer to thee is, God; when thou askest concerning the Trinity, thou gettest for answer, God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the spirit of man, when it cleaves to God, is one spirit, as the apostle openly declares, “He that is joined to the Lord is one spirit;” how much more is the equal Son, joined to the Father, together with Him one God!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear another testimony.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You know how many believed, when they sold all they had and laid it at the apostles’ feet, that it might be distributed to each according to his need; and what saith the Scripture of that gathering of the saints?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They had one soul and one heart in the Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If charity made one soul of so many souls, and one heart of so many hearts, how great must be the charity between the Father and the Son!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Surely it must be greater than that between those men who had one heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, the heart of many brethren was one by charity, if the soul of many brethren was one by charity, wouldst thou say that God the Father and God the Son are two?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If they are two Gods, there is not the highest charity between them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if charity is here so great as to make thy soul and thy friend’s soul one soul, how can it be then that the Father and the Son is not one God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Far be unfeigned faith from this thought.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In short, how excellent that charity is, understand hence: the souls of many men are many, and if they love one another, it is one soul; still, in the case of men, they may be called many souls, because the union is not so strong.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But there it is right for thee to say one God; two or three Gods it is not right for thee to say.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From this, the supreme and surpassing excellency of charity is shown thee to be such, that a greater cannot be.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For He whom God hath sent speaketh the words of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, of course, he said of Christ, to distinguish himself from Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did not God send John himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did he not say himself, “I am sent before Him”? and, “He that sent me to baptize with water”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And is it not of John that it is said, “Behold, I send my messenger before Thee, and he shall prepare Thy way”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does he not himself speak the words of God, he of whom it is said that he is more than a prophet?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then, if God sent him too, and he speaks the words of God, how do we understand him to have distinctly said of Christ, “He whom God hath sent speaketh the words of God”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But see what he adds: “For God giveth not the Spirit by measure.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this, “For God giveth not the Spirit by measure”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We find that God does give the Spirit by measure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear the apostle when he says, “According to the measure of the gift of Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To men He gives by measure, to the only Son He gives not by measure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How does He give to men by measure?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “To one is given by the Spirit the word of wisdom; to another the word of wisdom according to the same Spirit; to another faith by the same Spirit; to another prophecy; to another discerning of spirits; to another kinds of tongues; to another the gift of healing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are all apostles?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are all prophets?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are all teachers?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are all workers of miracles?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Have all the gift of healing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do all speak with tongues?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do all interpret?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This man has one gift, that man another; and what that man has, this has not: there is a measure, a certain division of gifts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To men, therefore, it is given by measure, and concord among them makes one body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As the hand receives one kind of gift to work, the eye another to see, the ear another to hear, the foot another to walk; nevertheless the soul that does all is one, in the hand to work, in the foot to walk, in the ear to hear, in the eye to see; so are also the gifts of believers diverse, distributed to them as to members, to each according to his proper measure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But Christ, who gives, receives not by measure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now hear further what follows: because He had said of the Son, “For God giveth not the Spirit by measure: the Father loveth the Son, and hath given all things into His hand,” He added, “hath given all things into His hands,” that thou mightest know also here with what distinction it is said, “The Father loveth the Son.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does the Father not love John?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet He has not given all things into his hand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does the Father not love Paul?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet He has not given all things into his hand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Father loveth the Son:” but as father loveth, not as master loveth a servant; as the Only Son, not as an adopted son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so “hath given all things into His hand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means “all things”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That the Son should be such as the Father is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To equality with Himself He begat Him in whom it was no robbery to be in the form of God, equal to God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Father loveth the Son, and hath given all things into His hand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, having deigned to send us the Son, let us not imagine that it is something less than the Father that is sent to us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father, in sending the Son, sent His other self.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the disciples, still thinking that the Father is something greater than the Son, seeing only the flesh, and not understanding His divinity, said to Him, “Lord, show us the Father and it sufficeth us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As much as to say, “We know Thee already, and bless Thee that we know Thee: for we thank Thee that Thou hast shown Thyself to us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But as yet we know not the Father: therefore our heart is inflamed, and occupied with a certain holy longing of seeing Thy Father who sent Thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Show us Him, and we shall desire nothing more of Thee: for it sufficeth us when He has been shown, than whom none can be greater.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A good longing, a good desire; but small intelligence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now the Lord Jesus Himself, regarding them as small men seeking great things, and Himself great among the small, and yet small among the small, says to Philip, one of the disciples, who had said this: “Am I so long time with you, and ye have not known me, Philip?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here Philip might have answered, Thee we have known, but did we say to Thee, Show us Thyself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have known Thee, but it is the Father we seek to know.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He immediately adds, “He that hath seen me, hath seen the Father also.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, One equal with the Father has been sent, let us not estimate Him from the weakness of the flesh, but think of the majesty clothed in flesh, but not weighed down by the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, remaining God with the Father, He was made man among men, that, through Him who was made man, thou mightest become such as to receive God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For man could not receive God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Man could see man; God he could not apprehend.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why could he not apprehend God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because he had not the eye of the heart, by which to apprehend Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There was something within disordered, something without sound: man had the eyes of the body sound, but the eyes of the heart sick.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was made man to the eye of the body; so that, believing on Him who could be seen in bodily form, thou mightst be healed for seeing Him whom thou wast not able to see spiritually.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Am I so long time with you, and ye know me not, Philip?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that hath seen me, hath seen the Father also.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why did they not see Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lo, they did see Him, and yet saw not the Father: they saw the flesh, but the majesty was concealed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What the disciples who loved Him saw, saw also the Jews who crucified Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Inwardly, then, was He all; and in such manner inwardly in the flesh, that He remained with the Father when He came to the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Carnal thought does not apprehend what I say: let it defer understanding, and begin by faith; let it hear what follows: “He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life: and he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has not said, The wrath of God cometh to him; but, “The wrath of God abideth on him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All that are born mortals have the wrath of God with them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What wrath of God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That wrath which Adam first received.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the first man sinned, and heard the sentence, “Thou shalt die the death,” he became mortal, and we began to be born mortal; and we have been born with the wrath of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From this stock came the Son, not having sin, and He was clothed with flesh and mortality.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He partook with us of the wrath of God, are we slow to partake with Him the grace of God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He, then, that will not believe the Son, on the same “the wrath of God abideth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What wrath of God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That of which the apostle says, “We also were by nature the children of wrath, even as the rest.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All are therefore children of wrath, because coming of the curse of death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Believe on Christ, for thee made mortal, that thou mayest receive Him, the immortal; and when thou shalt have received His immortality, thou shalt no longer be mortal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He lived, thou wast dead; He died that thou shouldst live.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has brought us the grace of God, and has taken away the wrath of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God has conquered death, lest death should conquer man.
